title: Server Action input validation order
body: Always safeParse FIRST before any auth check or DB call. Order: safeParse → getAuthUser() → role check → business logic. Prevents leaking auth status to unauthenticated callers.

title: Compensating saga for email+DB
body: Insert invitation row → try send email → on failure DELETE the row. True atomicity impossible across DB+HTTP. On email failure, compensate by rolling back the DB insert.

title: queryForOrg for all org-scoped queries
body: All org-scoped DB queries must use queryForOrg(orgId, userId, sql, params). Never use raw query() for org-scoped data. This enforces multi-tenancy at the DB layer.

title: Login form uses signIn not Server Action
body: The login form calls NextAuth signIn('credentials', {...}) directly — NOT a custom Server Action. Only signUp and acceptInvite use Server Actions. This is intentional — NextAuth manages session creation.

title: Shell architecture: Server wrappers + Client children
body: Sidebar and Topbar are Server Components that receive user props from the async layout and render Client Component children (SidebarNav, UserAvatarMenu, etc.). Server Components cannot be imported by Client Components — data flows one-way from Server to Client via props.

title: Async Server Component unit test pattern
body: DashboardLayout is async. In Vitest/JSDOM: call await DashboardLayout({children}) to get the JSX, then render(jsx). Mock getServerSession (next-auth/next), redirect (next/navigation), and child Server Components (Sidebar, Topbar) as simple stubs.

title: orgName in NextAuth JWT
body: orgName is NOT in NextAuth defaults. The authorize callback JOINs the orgs table (JOIN orgs o ON o.id = u.org_id) to fetch org_name. JWT callback writes token.orgName. Session callback reads token.orgName → session.user.orgName. Fallback gracefully to empty string if absent.
